rootCauseAnalysis.summary: The api-gateway Deployment is mounted with a misconfigured ConfigMap 'api-config-bad' that forces all API routes to return HTTP 500, causing 100% error rate and SLO error budget burn. The correct ConfigMap 'api-config' exists in the same namespace and returns HTTP 200 for all routes. Pods pass health probes (liveness/readiness on /healthz) but serve errors to all real traffic.
contributingFactors: Deployment spec references ConfigMap 'api-config-bad' (volume name 'config') instead of the correct 'api-config'
ConfigMap 'api-config-bad' explicitly configures /api/status and /api/ routes to return HTTP 500 with body 'internal server error'
Only /healthz returns HTTP 200, so liveness/readiness probes pass — pods appear healthy but serve errors to all real traffic
traffic-gen hits /api/status every 0.2 seconds, generating continuous error traffic that rapidly burns the SLO error budget
A rollout at ~17:01 UTC introduced 'api-config-bad'; a second rollout at ~17:06 UTC still left the bad ConfigMap in use
No prior remediation actions have been taken on this Deployment

kaResponse.analysis: The api-gateway Deployment is mounted with a misconfigured ConfigMap 'api-config-bad' that forces all API routes to return HTTP 500, causing 100% error rate and SLO error budget burn. The correct ConfigMap 'api-config' exists in the same namespace and returns HTTP 200 for all routes. Pods pass health probes (liveness/readiness on /healthz) but serve errors to all real traffic.
